titleStmt.title: Letter from W. O. Henry to Mr Kerford, 8 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: W. P. Henry

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from a Mr Henry to a Mr Kerford in which Kerford is asked to pass on a letter to Miss Ross so that she may communicate with the writer. A reply is written on the bottom of the page from Andrew Philip Magill (1871-1941), the private secretary of Chief Secretary of Ireland Augustine Birrell (1850-1933) claiming that Miss Ross had indeed written and will do so again.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/7557/1

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Mr. Magill
To sec
W. O. H.
8/5/16 Mr. Kerford, Please show
this letter to Miss Ross in
order that she may
communicate with the
writer. W. O. H.
8/5/16 Mr. Henry
Miss Ross has written &
will write again to her brother
soon. Returned with
thanks, A. P. M. 10/5 JBK 8/5/16
With
The Under Secretary's Compliments.
Dublin Castle
